name: MiseFlow

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🍳 MiseFlow 1.0.5 — Initial public release

> *Mise en place* — the culinary practice of preparing everything before you cook.

MiseFlow turns your Obsidian recipe notes into a full meal planning and grocery system. This is the first public release.

---

### Features

**Meal planning**
- Add recipes to your weekly plan with an optional day and meal type
- Choose exactly which ingredients to add to your grocery list — nothing is added blindly
- Your meal plan lives in a real vault note you can edit by hand

**Grocery list**
- Note-backed: stored as a readable Markdown checklist you can edit directly
- Smart quantity merging across recipes and manually added items
- Group by category, by recipe, by source, or as a flat list
- Add one-off items not tied to any recipe
- Export as plain text, Markdown checklist, or grouped by category

**Shopping Assistant panel**
- Check off items as you shop — state syncs to the grocery note
- See meals planned for the week with a link to the meal plan note
- Reset checks between trips

**Recipe view**
- Clean card view for cooking: hero image, portion multiplier, timing badges, diet tags, nutrition panel
- Meat temperature safety badges on relevant ingredients
- Allergen warning banner if a recipe matches your personal allergen list
- Optional high-GI ingredient badges with a configurable dictionary
- Mark as cooked (records date and increments cook count in frontmatter)
- Add to meal plan directly from the recipe view

**Recipe library**
- Meal suggester surfaces recipes you haven't cooked recently
- Cooking stats leaderboard
- Favourite recipes with one click

---

### Bug fixes in this release
- Fixed meat temperature badge stretching to full width in the recipe view
- Fixed cramped layout in the Add to meal plan modal

---

If you run into anything unexpected, please [open an issue](https://github.com/ATommasi/miseflow/issues).
